the fraction $\frac{100!}{10^{50}}$ is written in its irreducible form, and the denominator is determined by analyzing the prime factorization of 100! and the powers of 10 in the denominator. the prime factorization of 100! includes all the primes up to 100, and the denominator is $10^{50}$, which is $2^{50} \times 5^{50}$. the denominator of the irreducible fraction will be the product of the remaining prime factors after removing the factors of 2 and 5 from the numerator. the number of times 2 appears in 100! is 97, and the number of times 5 appears is 20. since the denominator has 50 factors of 5, and the numerator has 20, the remaining factors of 5 in the denominator are 10. similarly, the number of factors of 2 in the numerator is 97, and the denominator has 50, so the remaining factors of 2 in the denominator are 47. the denominator of the irreducible fraction is therefore $2^{47} \times 5^{10}$. this is the final answer. ***
